title: Expand The AI Layer With New Domain Scenarios
goal: Extend the existing AI runtime/gateway contract beyond the current core work order flows so the AI layer can safely cover more real project scenarios using code-enforced tools, task types, confirmation rules, and verification gates.
principles: Prefer bounded slices over one-shot broad refactors.
Do not add speculative scenarios that are not grounded in current domain code.
Keep code-first source of truth for tools and generated JSON registry sync.
Preserve structured envelopes, trace propagation, and contract validation.
Any new write scenario must be confirmation-safe when risk justifies it.
global_constraints: Do not break the existing remediation foundation already committed on main.
Do not edit config/ai/tools.json manually without syncing from Python definitions.
Do not remove or weaken PendingAction-based confirmation for existing write tools.
Do not introduce new chat frontends or unrelated UI redesign.
Keep AGENTS.md and PROJECT_HANDOFF.md aligned if behavior or workflow expectations materially change.
global_verification: ./.venv/bin/python manage.py test apps.ai apps.core apps.workorders apps.inventory apps.analytics
./.venv/bin/python -m py_compile services/agent_runtime/*.py apps/ai/*.py apps/core/*.py apps/workorders/*.py apps/inventory/*.py apps/analytics/*.py

slices.objective: Add a first-class executable task type for reading one work order in detail so the AI layer can reason about detail retrieval explicitly instead of treating it as an undeclared tool call.
slices.why_now: The tool already exists as workorders.get, but there is no declared task type for this scenario, which keeps part of the runtime behavior partially implicit.
expected_changes: Extend config/ai/task_types.json with a detail lookup task type.
Extend services/agent_runtime/task_types.py with an executable contract for the detail scenario.
Ensure task_type_report and slot_state cover work order id / number lookup variants.
Add tests for contract resolution, slot coverage, and task_type_report behavior.

slices.objective: Expose closure confirmation and post-closure rating as explicit AI scenarios grounded in existing work order domain services.
slices.why_now: The domain already has confirm_closure and rating behavior, but the AI layer currently cannot orchestrate these workflows safely or explicitly.
expected_changes: Add canonical Python tool definitions for closure confirmation and rating.
Implement Django gateway dispatch paths using existing policy/service logic.
Apply confirmation semantics only where appropriate based on actual domain risk.
Add executable task type contracts and tests.
Update generated config/ai/tools.json via sync command.

slices.objective: Expand the AI layer from inventory lookup into real inventory management scenarios already supported by the project UI and policy layer.
slices.why_now: Inventory has create, update, archive flows in the app, but the AI layer currently only supports devices.list as a lookup tool.
expected_changes: Add canonical Python tool definitions for inventory device create/update/archive.
Implement Django-side service and gateway execution paths using existing model/form semantics and manage-inventory policy.
Use PendingAction for destructive or materially mutating inventory actions where risk warrants confirmation.
Add task type contracts and slot validation for inventory scenarios.
Add focused tests for permission, confirmation, and structured responses.

slices.objective: Add read-only AI scenarios for analytics summaries already computed by the analytics dashboard.
slices.why_now: Analytics is already present in the project and is a natural low-risk AI scenario because it is read-only.
expected_changes: Add a read-only analytics tool that returns structured aggregates already present in the dashboard.
Represent analytics task types as executable contracts.
Keep output machine-readable and avoid formatting-only payloads.
Add tests for structure and contract validation.

slices.objective: Close the block by hardening validation/docs around the newly added scenarios so the expanded AI layer remains code-first and maintainable.
slices.why_now: New scenarios increase drift risk unless the registry, validation, and handoff docs are updated as part of the same block.
expected_changes: Extend semantic validators if the new scenarios require additional alignment checks.
Regenerate tool registry and ensure contracts stay green.
Update AGENTS.md and PROJECT_HANDOFF.md only where the new scenarios materially affect agent workflow or AI capabilities.
Leave a clear final state in this JSON plan by marking slice statuses completed or blocked with brief notes.

completion_rule: The block is complete only when every slice status is completed, global verification passes, and the expanded AI surface remains aligned with AGENTS.md and PROJECT_HANDOFF.md.
